title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Achieving the Sustainable Development Goals

title: A Deep Learning Based Framework to Identify Undocumented Orphaned Oil and Gas Wells from Historical Maps: A Case Study for California and Oklahoma
description: A neural network model identifies undocumented orphaned wells, helping locate methane-leaking sites and aiding environmental monitoring efforts.

title: Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning
description: Machine learning can help combat climate change by reducing emissions and aiding adaptation through applications like smart grids and disaster management.
sub_domain: AI for Climate Mitigation Strategies

title: The Green Mirage: Impact of Location- and Market-Based Carbon Intensity Estimation on Carbon Optimization Efficacy
description: Organizations are reducing electricity-related carbon emissions by adjusting energy use based on carbon intensity data and securing renewable energy through power purchase agreements.
sub_domain: Carbon Accounting for AI Operations

title: The First Ice-Free Day in the Arctic Ocean Could Occur Before 2030
description: New projections suggest the Arctic's first ice-free day could occur before 2030, driven by rapid ice loss and strong seasonal warming.
sub_domain: AI for Arctic Climate Projections

title: Unexpected Warming From Land Radiative Management
description: Land radiative management cools targeted areas but unintentionally increases temperatures in surrounding regions by reducing precipitation.
sub_domain: AI in Geoengineering and Climate Effects

title: Big Data (R)evolution in Geography: Complexity Modelling in the Last Two Decades
description: Big Data offers vast potential for improving simulation modeling in geography, but data availability and verification challenges must be addressed.
sub_domain: Big Data and Geospatial AI

title: Evaluation of Effects on a Viaduct Affected by a Snowmelt-Induced Landslide Using AI-Driven Pattern Recognition
description: Machine learning enhances landslide hazard assessment by linking snowmelt patterns to infrastructure deformation, improving predictive accuracy and risk management.
sub_domain: Snowmelt and Landslides with AI-Pattern Recongition

title: Using AI to Detect Climate Tipping Points Or Why It’s Hard to Understand Rapid Changes in the Earth System
description: AI can help identify climate tipping points by simplifying Earth system dynamics and isolating key mechanisms driving rapid environmental shifts.
sub_domain: AI for Climate Tipping Point Analysis

title: Neuro-Symbolic Bi-Directional Translation -- Deep Learning Explainability for Climate Tipping Point Research
description: Neuro-symbolic AI improves explainability in deep learning climate models by translating scientific questions into executable programs for tipping point analysis.
sub_domain: AI for Climate Tipping Point Research

title: Generating Physically-Consistent Satellite Imagery for Climate Visualizations
description: Physics-conditioned generative models improve the accuracy of AI-synthesized satellite imagery, reducing hallucinations in climate-related visualizations.
journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
sub_domain: AI-Generated Climate Visualizations

title: Resolution-Agnostic Transformer-Based Climate Downscaling
description: A pretrained Earth Vision Transformer enables cost-efficient climate model downscaling, improving regional weather predictions for planning and adaptation.
sub_domain: Climate-Modeling Using Transformer-Based Climate Downscaling

title: The Growing Energy Footprint of Artificial Intelligence
description: AI saw rapid expansion in 2022–2023, driven by the success of ChatGPT and major tech companies launching their own generative AI chatbots.
sub_domain: Rising Power Consumption of AI Models

title: The Unpaid Toll: Quantifying the Public Health Impact of AI
description: AI's growing energy demand worsens air quality, imposing significant public health costs, especially on disadvantaged communities, necessitating better reporting and mitigation strategies.
sub_domain: Public Health and AI’s Environmental Burden

title: Detecting and Attributing Change in Climate and Complex Systems: Foundations, Green’s Functions, and Nonlinear Fingerprints
description: Optimal fingerprinting in climate science links observed changes to human and natural causes, with response theory enhancing its accuracy and broader applicability.
sub_domain: Climate and Complex Systems and Change

title: 3D Cloud Reconstruction Through Geospatially-Aware Masked Autoencoders
description: Self-supervised learning enhances 3D cloud reconstruction, improving climate predictions by reducing uncertainties in climate models.
sub_domain: Reconstructing Clouds with Geospatially-Aware AI Models

title: Potential of Artificial Intelligence in Reducing Energy and Carbon Emissions of Commercial Buildings at Scale
description: AI can enhance building energy efficiency, potentially reducing carbon emissions by up to 90% by 2050 when combined with policy and low-carbon energy.
sub_domain: AI in Energy Efficiency for Buildings

title: Combining Climate Models and Observations to Predict the Time Remaining Until Regional Warming Thresholds Are Reached
description: Machine learning improves regional climate predictions, estimating 1.5°C warming by 2040 and demonstrating the value of transfer learning for climate modeling.
sub_domain: AI for Regional Climate Threshold Prediction

title: Deep Learning Waterways for Rural Infrastructure Development
description: AI-powered mapping with WaterNet improves waterway detection in underserved regions, aiding rural infrastructure development and humanitarian planning.
sub_domain: AI in Rural Water Infrastructure

title: A Deep Learning Approach to the Automated Segmentation of Bird Vocalizations from Weakly Labeled Crowd-Sourced Audio
description: Improved machine learning techniques enhance bird call detection in passive acoustic monitoring, boosting precision and biodiversity tracking in climate research.
sub_domain: Using AI to Detect Bird Vocalizations from Audio

title: Multi-Branch Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network For Efficient Ice Layer Thickness Prediction
description: A multi-branch spatio-temporal graph neural network improves ice layer prediction, enhancing accuracy in tracking polar ice sheet changes.
sub_domain: AI for Ice Thickness Forecasting

title: Temporal Sequence Transformer to Advance Long-Term Streamflow Prediction
description: Transformer models outperform LSTMs in streamflow prediction, improving hydrological forecasting for water resource management and climate adaptation.
sub_domain: AI in Hydrological Forecasting

title: Prithvi-EO-2.0: A Versatile Multi-Temporal Foundation Model for Earth Observation Applications
description: Prithvi-EO-2.0, a geospatial foundation model trained on NASA satellite data, outperforms its predecessor and other models in remote sensing tasks, aiding applications like disaster response and ecosystem monitoring.
sub_domain: AI Foundation Models for Earth Observation

title: NatureLM-Audio: an Audio-Language Foundation Model for Bioacoustics
description: NatureLM-audio, a language-audio model for bioacoustics, advances species detection and conservation by setting new benchmarks in animal vocalization analysis.
sub_domain: Using AI to Detect Bioacoustics with Audio-Language Models

title: Probabilistic Emulation of a Global Climate Model with Spherical DYffusion
description: A generative AI model integrates diffusion and neural operators to produce accurate, efficient 100-year climate simulations, advancing data-driven climate modeling.
sub_domain: Using AI to Emulate a Global Climate Model

title: A Scalable Method for Modulating Plant Gene Expression Using a Multispecies Genomic Model and Protoplast-Based Massively Parallel Reporter Assay
description: The CRE.AI.TIVE platform uses machine learning to enhance plant gene regulation, enabling scalable gene upregulation without prior knowledge of regulatory elements.
sub_domain: Using AI in Modulating Plant Gene Expressions with Agriculture

title: Sustainable AI: Environmental Implications, Challenges and Opportunities
description: This paper provides a holistic analysis of AI's environmental impact, examining the carbon footprint across data, algorithms, and hardware, while proposing hardware-software optimizations and industry best practices to reduce AI’s overall carbon footprint.
sub_domain: Broader Sustainability Issues in AI

title: Estimating the Carbon Footprint of Bloom, A 176B Parameter Language Model
description: The life cycle carbon footprint of BLOOM, a 176-billion parameter language model, is quantified, revealing emissions from training, hardware manufacturing, and real-time inference, while highlighting challenges in accurate carbon reporting.
sub_domain: Carbon Footprint of Large AI Models

title: Improvements on Phase Classification Using Atmospheric Melting and Refreezing Energy Based on Soundings
description: A new precipitation phase classification method using upper-level melting and refreezing energy improves accuracy in distinguishing snow and rain, especially in temperature inversion conditions, outperforming previous approaches in North America and enhancing satellite snowfall retrievals.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Phase Classifications of Matter

title: Environmental Burden of United States Data Centers in the Artificial Intelligence Era
description: US data centers, driven by AI adoption, consumed over 4% of the nation’s electricity in 2023, with 56% from fossil fuels, emitting more than 105 million tons of CO2e, exceeding the national average carbon intensity by 48%.
sub_domain: Energy and Emissions of AI Data Centers

title: Samudra: An AI Global Ocean Emulator for Climate
description: The ocean emulator Samudra models key ocean variables with high stability and interannual accuracy, running 150 times faster than traditional climate models, though it requires further refinement to capture forcing trends accurately.
sub_domain: AI Climate Emulation Models

title: Building Ocean Climate Emulators
description: Ocean emulator development is still in its early stages, but this study establishes key guidelines by demonstrating stable surface field predictions for over eight years and exploring the role of atmospheric interactions and variable representation in emulator design.
sub_domain: AI for Ocean Climate Model Emulators

title: Data-Driven Predictions of Peak Warming Under Rapid Decarbonization
description: Convolutional neural networks predict that even under ambitious decarbonization scenarios, global warming is highly likely to exceed 1.5°C, with a strong probability of record-breaking annual temperatures surpassing 2023, posing significant risks from extreme local conditions.
authors: Noah S. Diffenbaugh, Elizabeth A. Barnes
sub_domain: Using AI to Predict Peak Warming with Decarbonization

title: Combining Climate Models and Observations to Predict the Time Remaining Until Regional Warming Thresholds Are Reached
description: A machine learning approach using convolutional neural networks and transfer learning predicts that most regions will surpass 1.5°C warming by 2040, with many exceeding 2.0°C by the same time, improving regional climate projections by integrating observational data with climate models.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Prediction Times Until Peak Warming

title: Leveraging the Collaborative Power of AI and Citizen Science for Sustainable Development
description: Integrating citizen science into AI can enhance sustainability efforts by addressing AI challenges like social bias while leveraging synergies for improved monitoring and progress toward sustainable development.
sub_domain: Citizen Science and AI for Sustainability

title: The Potential for AI to Revolutionize Conservation: A Horizon Scan
description: A horizon scan with conservation scientists and AI experts identifies 21 impactful AI applications for biodiversity conservation, including digital twins and AI-powered advisors, emphasizing benefits while acknowledging the need for equitable and complementary integration with traditional methods.
sub_domain: AI for Biodiversity Conservation

title: Increasing Behavioral Richness and Managing Structural Uncertainty in Social-Ecological System Agent-Based Models
description: Integrating diverse decision models into agent-based models (ABMs) enhances the realism of adaptation decision-making in climate change scenarios, showing that behavioral heterogeneity can introduce as much or more uncertainty than economic or environmental factors while improving predictive accuracy.
sub_domain: Using AI-Agent Models to model Socio-Economic Impacts in Society

title: AI  and  Machine  Learning  in  Optoelectronics  for Global  Sustainability
description: AI and ML optimize optoelectronic systems for sustainability by enhancing energy efficiency, renewable energy harvesting, environmental monitoring, and smart cities while also advancing ocean optics and photonics for marine ecosystem monitoring and climate change mitigation.
sub_domain: Using AI and ML in Optoelectronics to Model Sustainability Impacts

title: Using AI to Assess Corporate Climate Transition Disclosures
description: A Large Language Model-based tool assesses company transition plans toward a low-carbon economy, revealing a gap between target-setting disclosures and concrete implementation while highlighting greenwashing risks and the need for enhanced scrutiny.
sub_domain: Using AI with Corporations for Climate Disclosures

title: Forecasting Thailand’s Transportation CO2 Emissions: A Comparison Among Artificial Intelligent Models
description: Artificial neural networks (ANN) outperform other models in forecasting transportation-related CO2 emissions, highlighting their reliability for policy evaluation, with scenario analysis showing significant emission reductions under the “30@30” policy by 2030.
sub_domain: Forecasting Thailand's CO2 Emissions in the Transportation Sector Using Different AI Models

title: Upper Ocean Thermodynamic Response to Coupling Currents to Wind Stress over the Gulf Stream
description: Including current feedback in high-resolution atmosphere-ocean model simulations over the Gulf Stream extension alters upper ocean heat transport, aligning temperature patterns with vorticity and reducing submesoscale vertical heat flux, impacting ocean-atmosphere energy transfer and stratification.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Ocean Responses to Wind Stress in the Gulf Stream

title: A Comparative Analysis of Text-to-Image Generative AI Models in Scientific Contexts: A Case Study on Nuclear Power
description: Generative AI tools, such as DALL-E and DreamStudio, show potential in improving public engagement and energy literacy about low-carbon sources like nuclear energy, though they fall short in representing technical details, addressing biases, and accurately depicting indigenous landscapes.
sub_domain: Using AI in Scientific Contexts with Nuclear Power Being An Example

title: Global Database of Cement Production Assets and Upstream Suppliers
description: A globally consolidated asset-level dataset for cement production, incorporating plant age and raw material sourcing, enhances emissions tracking, revealing inefficiencies in supply chains while leveraging geospatial computer vision and Large Language Models for comprehensive industry analysis.
sub_domain: Using AI in Cement Production Ecosystem Mapping

title: Automating the Search for Artificial Life with Foundation Models
description: Foundation models (FMs) revolutionize Artificial Life (ALife) research by automating the discovery of diverse and novel life-like simulations across multiple substrates, enabling breakthroughs in open-ended evolution and human-aligned quantification of complex phenomena.
sub_domain: Using AI to Find Artifical Life Using Foundation Models

title: Complex Multivariate Model Predictions for Coral Diversity with Climatic Change
description: Coral reef future projections using boosted regression tree models reveal that human influence variables predict coral diversity better than temperature alone, with finer-scale environmental interactions playing a crucial role in moderating climate change impacts.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Coral Diversity with Climate Impacts

title: Abrupt, Climate-Induced Increase in Wildfires in British Columbia Since the Mid-2000s
description: Wildfire activity in British Columbia has surged since 2005 due to rising temperatures and increased evaporative demand, reversing a century-long decline despite high precipitation levels, with climate change and land-use factors intensifying fire regimes.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Wildfire Detection in British Columbia Since the Mid-2000s

title: Machine Learning for Climate Physics and Simulations
description: Machine learning accelerates climate knowledge and simulations by integrating data-driven techniques with climate physics, emphasizing the need for interpretability in small-data scenarios and interdisciplinary collaboration to enhance model reliability.
sub_domain: Using AI for Climate Physics and Simulations

title: The Making of Critical Data Center Studies
description: Data centers have become central to interdisciplinary scholarship as both infrastructure and metaphor, giving rise to Critical Data Center Studies, which examine their social, political, cultural, and environmental impacts beyond mere technical functionality.
sub_domain: The Making of Data Centers and AI

title: Predicting Climate Change Using an Autoregressive Long Short-Term Memory Model
description: A long short-term memory-based machine learning model successfully predicts climate trends and seasonal patterns over long periods, demonstrating its potential as a viable alternative to traditional mathematical models for climate forecasting.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Climate Change Using an Autoregressive Memory Model

title: Artificial Intelligence Potential for Net Zero Sustainability: Current Evidence and Prospects
description: AI plays a transformative role in achieving net-zero emissions by optimizing energy systems, enhancing climate modeling, and improving sustainability across sectors, while challenges in data, ethics, and governance must be addressed for scalable and effective implementation.
sub_domain: Mapping AI's Pathway to Net-Zero Sustainability

title:
Robustness and Visual Explanation for Black Box Image, Video, and ECG Signal
Classification with Reinforcement Learning
description: A reinforcement learning framework optimizes adversarial attacks across ECG, image, and video classification models, outperforming state-of-the-art methods while enhancing interpretability and resilience through superior localization masks and adversarial training.
sub_domain: Using AI in Reinforcement Learning and Model Classification

title: Hierarchical Multi-Agent Control Framework for Energy Efficiency and Carbon Emission Reduction in Multi-Zone Buildings
description: A scalable multi-agent control system for smart building HVAC optimization reduces energy consumption by 8.8% and carbon emissions by 23.4%, offering a flexible alternative to centralized approaches by balancing comfort and efficiency through coordinated local agents.
sub_domain: Using AI Agents in Carbon Emission Reductions for Buildings

title: Feasibility Test of Per-Flight Contrail Avoidance in Commercial Aviation
description: A randomized controlled trial demonstrates that per-flight contrail avoidance in commercial aviation is feasible, reducing contrail formation by 64% using altitude adjustments based on machine learning and physics-based predictions, though with a 2% increase in fuel consumption.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Contrail Avoidance in Commerical Aviation

title: AI for Climate Change: Unveiling Pathways to Sustainable Development Through GHG Predictions
description: A comprehensive evaluation of machine learning regression models enhances the precision of GHG emission predictions, comparing traditional and advanced algorithms while integrating feature selection techniques like LIME to improve model interpretability and inform environmental policy.
sub_domain: Greenhouse Gas Emission Predictions using AI

title: Green Liminals: Feasibility Study for AI-Driven Budget Optimization for Urban Sustainable Solutions
description: The project explores innovative ways to address Barcelona’s sustainability challenges, such as carbon emissions, air pollution, and energy inefficiency. By leveraging AI, IoT sensors, and Nature-Based Solutions (NBS), the project aims to optimize municipal budgets for interventions that transform underutilized urban spaces into productive assets.
sub_domain: Using AI Solutions for Urban Sustainability

title:
Measuring How AI Innovations and Financial Accessibility Influence Environmental Sustainability in the G-7: The Role of Globalization with
Panel ARDL and Quantile Regression Analysis
description: AI innovation positively impacts environmental sustainability in the G-7 region, while globalization and urbanization have negative effects, with findings confirming the Load Capacity Curve hypothesis and revealing bidirectional causality between AI innovation and environmental load capacity.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Environmental Sustainability With The G7

title: AIFS -- ECMWF's Data-Driven Forecasting System
description: The Artificial Intelligence Forecasting System (AIFS), developed by ECMWF, leverages graph neural networks and transformers to produce highly skilled medium-range weather forecasts, running alongside traditional numerical models and providing open-access predictions.
sub_domain: AI and Weather Modeling for Europe

title: Technology at the Table: Incorporating AI into Contemporary Food Industry Operations
description: AI enhances food and agriculture by optimizing supply chains, improving quality, and ensuring safety, but its adoption faces challenges related to data privacy, automation-driven job displacement, and ethical concerns in sustainability and regulation compliance.
sub_domain: Using AI for Food Industry Operations

title: Sustainable AI Regulations
description: Current proposals for AI regulation, in the EU and beyond, aim to spur AI that is trustworthy (e.g., AI Act) and accountable (e.g., AI Liability) What is missing, however, is a robust regulatory discourse and roadmap to make AI, and technology more broadly, environmentally sustainable. This paper aims to take first steps to fill this gap.
sub_domain: Regulatory Approaches to AI Sustainability

title: The Carbon Emissions of Writing and Illustrating Are Lower for AI Than for Humans
description: AI systems generate significantly lower carbon emissions than humans for writing and illustrating tasks, emitting 130–1500 times less CO2e per text page and 310–2900 times less per image, though social and legal impacts remain unaccounted for.
sub_domain: Using AI to Compare Carbon Emissions of Actions and Human Beings

title: Textbooks Are All You Need
description: Phi-1, a compact 1.3B parameter Transformer-based language model for code, achieves strong performance on HumanEval (50.6%) and MBPP (55.5%) despite its small scale, benefiting from high-quality web data and GPT-3.5-generated training materials.
sub_domain: Using AI Models and Computer Performance for Code

title: Carbon Connect: An Ecosystem for Sustainable Computing
description: ICT contributes 2.1%–3.9% of global GHG emissions, necessitating a 45% reduction by 2030, requiring interdisciplinary collaboration to develop sustainable computing solutions that balance environmental impact with implementation costs.
sub_domain: AI for Carbon-Aware Computing

title: Carbon Explorer: A Holistic Framework for Designing Carbon Aware Datacenters
description: Carbon Explorer optimizes 24/7 carbon-free datacenter operations by balancing renewable energy sources, storage, and workload scheduling, addressing hourly carbon intensity challenges and enabling location-based sustainability strategies.
sub_domain: Using a Holistic Framework for Carbon-Aware Datacenters

title: Carbon Emissions and Large Neural Network Training
description:
The computation demand for machine learning (ML) has grown rapidly recently, which comes with a
number of costs. Estimating the energy cost helps measure its environmental impact and finding greener
strategies, yet it is challenging without detailed information.
sub_domain: AI Model Training and Carbon Emissions

title: Enhancing Green Computing Through Energy-Aware Training: An Early-Stopping Perspective
description: Early stopping in deep learning training significantly reduces energy consumption and computational costs without sacrificing accuracy, aligning with green computing principles and optimizing efficiency in transfer learning models like MobileNetV2, InceptionV3, ResNet50V2, and Xception.
sub_domain: Green Computing Through Energy-Aware Training

title: Automated Cetacean Detection in UAV Imagery Using AI models: A Case Study on Delphinid Species
description: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) effectively automate cetacean detection from UAV aerial images, achieving high accuracy (83.9%) and improving conservation monitoring, while LSTM-based video models struggle with generalization, highlighting challenges in real-world application.
journal: International Journal of Data Science and Analytics
sub_domain: Using AI Models with Automated Cetacean Detection in UAV Imagery

title: Justice and Ethics in Conservation Remote Sensing: Current Discourses and Research Needs
description: Remote sensing in biodiversity conservation raises ethical and social justice concerns, including surveillance, power asymmetries, and militarization, necessitating lessons from AI ethics, formal oversight mechanisms, and further research on its political and geopolitical implications.
sub_domain: Conservation Remote Sensing with Justice an Ethics

title: The Gendered Forest: Digital Surveillance Technologies for Conservation and Gender-Environment Relationships
description: Biodiversity conservation in India's Corbett Tiger Reserve shapes gendered forest interactions, as digital surveillance technologies like drones and camera traps masculinize forest governance, reinforcing patriarchal norms and altering women's cultural and livelihood practices.
sub_domain: Conservation and Gender-Environment Relationships with Digital Surveillance

title: Data Justice and Biodiversity Conservation
description: The justice implications of conservation data are often overlooked, prompting a proposed framework addressing data composition, control, access, processing, and consequences to mitigate social and ecological harms and promote ethically and ecologically sound conservation strategies.
sub_domain: Biodiversity Conservation and Data Justice

title: Deep Learning and Satellite Remote Sensing for Biodiversity Monitoring and Conservation
description: Deep learning combined with satellite remote sensing enhances biodiversity monitoring and conservation by leveraging big data, but challenges in capacity building, data access, environmental costs, and model interpretability must be addressed for broader adoption.
sub_domain: Deep Learning and Satellite Remote Sensing for Biodiversity Conservation

title: A Novel Deep Learning-Based Bioacoustic Approach for Identification of Look-Alike White-Eye (Zosterops) Species Traded in Wildlife Markets
description: A deep learning-based bioacoustic AI tool enables citizen scientists and law enforcement to accurately identify traded songbird species in Asian markets, achieving 91.6% accuracy and supporting conservation efforts against overexploitation and illegal wildlife trade.
sub_domain: Trading Species in Wildlife Markets Using Deep Learning Approaches

title: Artificial Intelligence-Assisted Environmental DNA Metabarcoding and High-Resolution Underwater Optical Imaging for Noninvasive and Innovative Marine Environmental Monitoring
description: Artificial intelligence, environmental DNA metabarcoding, and high-resolution underwater optical imaging are advancing noninvasive marine environmental monitoring, offering scalable and automated solutions for conservation, particularly in fragile ecosystems like coral reefs.
sub_domain: Using AI DNA Metabarcoding for Marine Environmental Monitoring

title: Detection And Growth Estimation Of Indo–Pacific Eel (Anguilla marmorata Quoy & Gaimard, 1824) Using Machine Learning In Central Vietnam
description: Overfishing and habitat loss threaten the Indo-Pacific eel (Anguilla marmorata), necessitating conservation efforts, with research focusing on modeling its spatial distribution and growth in relation to environmental factors.
journal: Machine Learning, Biodiversity Monitoring, Indo-Pacific Eel
sub_domain: Machine Learning in Central Vietnam for the Indo-Pacific Eel Conservation Efforts

title: Explainable Machine Learning (XML) Framework for Seismic Assessment of Structures Using Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost)
description: A machine learning-driven framework integrating XGBoost and Spring-Rotational Friction Dampers enhances seismic resilience by optimizing retrofitting strategies, using eXplainable AI (XAI) methods to improve transparency and decision-making in structural engineering.
sub_domain: Using a Machine Learning Framework for Seismic Assessment of Structures

title: Assessing the Size of Spatial Extreme Events Using Local Coefficients Based on Excursion Sets
description: A new family of spatial-extent coefficients assesses extreme georeferenced events by measuring spatial spread from threshold exceedances, with a semiparametric model enabling statistical extrapolation, demonstrated through simulations and gridded temperature data in France.
sub_domain: Modeling Spatial Extreme Events Using Machine Learning

title:
Investigating the Impact of Internet of Things and Artificial Intelligence on Environmental Sustainability Performance (Case
Study: Hotel Industry)
description: The Internet of Things and artificial intelligence enhance environmental sustainability in the hotel industry by improving green supply chain management, with structural equation modeling confirming their positive impact on efficiency and sustainability performance.
sub_domain: Using IoT and AI to Model Hotel Industry Sustainability

title: Pervasive Glacier Retreats Across Svalbard From 1985 to 2023
description: Calving front retreat of marine-terminating glaciers in Svalbard from 1985 to 2023, analyzed using deep learning, reveals seasonal and interannual variability driven by ocean warming, with future retreats expected to intensify due to increasing atmospheric blocking and regional warming.
journal: Glacier retreat, Spatial Variability, Machine Learning
sub_domain: Modeling Glacier Retreats in Svalbard Using AI

title: The Promise and Peril of Deploying Artificial Intelligence for Climate Security
description: AI has the potential to enhance climate security interventions but faces challenges related to data quality, algorithmic biases, and transparency, requiring integration into global climate governance and strengthened regulatory measures for effective implementation.
journal: AI, Peace and Security, Human Security
sub_domain: Risks and Benefits of Climate Security with AI Systems

title: Assessment of Climate Change Impact on Water Resources Using Machine Learning Algorithms
description: Machine learning enhances climate change impact assessments on water resources by downscaling Global Climate Models, analyzing hydroclimatic variations, and evaluating effects on rivers, groundwater, and water supply systems.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Using Machine Learning to Assess Climate Impact on Water Resources

title: Analysis Of Past And Projected Changes In Extreme Precipitation Indices In Some Watersheds In Côte D'Ivoire
description: Analysis of rainfall extremes in Côte d'Ivoire's watersheds (1976–2050) reveals decreasing drought indices in historical data but increasing flood-related indices under RCP 4.5 and RCP 8.5 scenarios, highlighting growing climate-induced disaster risks.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Past, Present, and Future Extreme Precipitation Events in Cote D'Ivoire

title: A Case Study Of An Extreme Flooding Episode In Charikar, Eastern Afghanistan
description: Severe flash floods in Charikar, Afghanistan, on August 26, 2020, were driven by extreme atmospheric instability, deep low-level convergence, and local topography, with climate change intensifying frontal activity and baroclinicity in the region.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Extreme Flooding in Charikar, Afghanistan

title: Multivariate Multi-Step LSTM Model For Flood Runoff Prediction: A Case Study On The Godavari River Basin In India
description: A deep-learning regression model using LSTM effectively predicts flood runoff in India's Godavari River, outperforming other time-series models and demonstrating potential for enhancing early flood warning systems.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Flood Runoff Predictions in the Godavari River Basin

title: Effects Of Climate Change On Streamflow In The Dez Basin Of Iran Using The IHACRES Model Based On The CMIP6 Model
description: This study uses the LARS-WG downscaling model and IHACRES hydrological model to project climate change impacts on river flow, showing varying rainfall and flow changes in the studied basin under different climate scenarios.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Climate Change Streamflow in The Dez Basin of Iran

title: Bias Correction And Projection Of Temperature Over The Altitudes Of The Upper Indus Basin Under CMIP6 Climate Scenarios From 1985 To 2100
description: This study uses a bias-correction technique on CMIP6 GCMs to project temperature changes in the Upper Indus Basin, revealing elevation-dependent warming and significant temperature increases from 2015 to 2100.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Climate Scenarios in The Upper Indus Basin

title: Hydroclimatic Projection: Statistical Learning And Downscaling Model For Rainfall And Runoff Forecasting
description: Climate change projections for the study area predict reduced rainfall and surface runoff, indicating a future water-stressed scenario, especially under SSP (Shared Socioeconomic Pathways) 4.5 and SSP 8.5 scenarios.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Rainfall and Runoff Forecasting with Climatic Projections

title: Reason For Less Water Supply Shortages Under Climate Change Condition: Evaluation Of Future Rainfall Data
description: Future rainfall data in Korea may underestimate multi-year droughts, highlighting limitations for evaluating long-term water supply resilience, particularly in the Boryeong Dam basin.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Evaluating Future Rainfall Data Using Climate Scenarios

title: Investigating The Projected Changes In Water Balance Components Under Climate Change Considering The Effect Of Storage Structures
description: This study highlights the impact of climate change on water balance in the Upper Bhima River, revealing reduced monsoon precipitation and baseflow sensitivity, with implications for agriculture, biodiversity, and water management.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Climate Change Effects on Storage Structures of Water Resources

title: Application Of Machine Learning Models In Assessing The Hydrological Changes Under Climate Change In The Transboundary 3S River Basin
description: Machine learning models predict hydrological changes in the 3S River Basin under climate change scenarios, showing increased flood risk in some areas and reduced streamflow in others.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Hydrological Chnages in The Transboundary 3S River Basin

title: Bias Correction Of ERA5-Land Temperature Data Using Standalone And Ensemble Machine Learning Models: A Case Of Northern Italy
description: Machine learning-based ensemble models improve bias correction in climate reanalysis data, enhancing temperature accuracy for water resource and climate studies.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Northern Italy and Machine Learning for Temperature Data

title: Statistical Analysis Of Climate Change Over Hanumangarh District
description: Statistical and graphical analyses of precipitation trends in Rajasthan reveal seasonal variability, aiding water resource planning for drought and flood management.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Climate Change Modeling in the Hanumangarh District

title: Assessing The Utility Of Hybrid Hydrological Modeling Over Complex Conditions Of The Chitral Basin, Pakistan
description: A hybrid AI and hydrological model improves streamflow forecasting in Pakistan’s Chitral basin, though data scarcity limits AI’s effectiveness in uncertain conditions.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Modeling Hydrological Changes in Chitral Basin, Pakistan

title: Forecasting Of Water Quality Parameters Of Sandia Station In Narmada Basin, Central India, Using AI Techniques
description: AI models effectively predict water quality changes due to climate impacts, aiding resource management and future risk assessment.
journal: Journal of Water and Climate Change
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Water Quality Parameters in Narmada Basin, India

title: Soft Computing Paradigm For Climate Change Adaptation And Mitigation In Iran, Pakistan, And Turkey: A Systematic Review
description: AI applications for climate resilience in Iran, Pakistan, and Turkey focus on water management, climate prediction, and disaster response, though challenges remain in data availability and collaboration.
sub_domain: Using AI to Model Climate Change in Iran, Pakistan, and Turkey

title: From Efficiency Gains to Rebound Effects: The Problem of Jevons' Paradox in AI's Polarized Environmental Debate
description: AI's climate impact extends beyond direct emissions, with efficiency gains often increasing resource consumption, necessitating interdisciplinary analysis and policy interventions to mitigate rebound effects.
sub_domain: Discussing Jevons' Paradox in AI's Environmental Debate

title: A Systematic Literature Review On Artificial Intelligence In Transforming Precision Agriculture For Sustainable Farming: Current Status And Future Directions
description: AI enhances precision agriculture through data-driven crop management, irrigation optimization, and pest control, but gaps remain in nutrient management and sensor integration.
sub_domain: Modeling Sustainable Farming with Precision Agriculture and AI

title: Large Language Models In Climate And Sustainability Policy: Limits And Opportunities
description: Natural language processing enhances climate policy analysis by processing and summarizing diverse texts, though human intervention remains crucial for effective knowledge utilization.
sub_domain: LLM Development in Climate and Sustainability Policy

title: Towards Energy Efficient RAN: From Industry Standards to Trending Practice
description: Optimizing 5G radio access networks through acceleration, consolidation, and shared infrastructure enhances energy efficiency while reducing costs and improving performance.
sub_domain: Energy Efficient Modeling with Industry Standards and Practices

title: Exploring The Sustainable Scaling Of AI Dilemma: A Projective Study Of Corporations' AI Environmental Impacts
description: AI's growing energy demands require standardized environmental assessments, transparency, and coordinated efforts across the value chain to align with net-zero goals.
sub_domain: Sustainable Scaling and AI Dillemmas with Environmental Impacts of Corporations

title: Deep Learning Model Based Prediction Of Vehicle CO2 Emissions With EXplainable AI Integration For Sustainable Environment
description: A deep learning model with explainable AI predicts vehicle CO2 emissions, aiding strategies for reducing transportation-related environmental impacts.
sub_domain: Modeling Vehicle CO2 Emissions with EXplainable AI Integration

title: A Machine Learning Model Using The Snapshot Ensemble Approach For Soil Respiration Prediction In An Experimental Oak Forest
description: A hybrid machine learning model improves soil respiration predictions, enhancing carbon cycle modeling and greenhouse gas management strategies.
sub_domain: Modeling Soil Respiration Prediction in An Experimental Oak Forest

title: Hype, Sustainability, and the Price of the Bigger-is-Better Paradigm in AI
description: The "bigger-is-better" AI paradigm is unsustainable, environmentally costly, and centralizes power, neglecting diverse applications and alternative research paths.
sub_domain: AI Sustainability and Paradigms

title: Softwarized Networks in the Age of Generative Artificial Intelligence: Use Cases, Challenges, and Opportunities
description: Generative AI could enhance software-defined networks by improving automation and flexibility, but significant challenges remain in realizing its full potential.
sub_domain: Softwarized Networks in the AI Gen Age

title: Limits to AI Growth: The Ecological and Social Consequences of Scaling
description: AI scaling faces technical, economic, ecological, and social limits, requiring a shift in priorities to prevent unsustainable growth and externalized harms.
sub_domain: Ecological Consequences of Scaling with AI Growth

title: Spatio-Temporal Load Shifting for Truly Clean Computing
description: Shifting datacenter workloads across time and location improves 24/7 carbon-free energy matching, enhancing clean computing efficiency and cost-effectiveness.
sub_domain: Spatio-Temporal Load Shifting for Clean Computing

title: Reanalysis-Based Global Radiative Response to Sea Surface Temperature Patterns: Evaluating The Ai2 Climate Emulator
description: Machine learning-based climate emulators can estimate radiative feedback sensitivity but require validation to ensure accurate representation of physical relationships.
sub_domain: Evaluating the Ai2 Climate Emulator

title: Algorithmic Environmentality: Data Infrastructures in Global Environmental Governance
description: "Algorithmic environmentality" describes the shift in global environmental governance toward data-driven solutions, assuming climate challenges stem from insufficient data rather than structural issues.
sub_domain: Data Infrastructures and Global Environmental Governance

title: Earth for AI: A Political Ecology of Data-Driven Climate Initiatives
description: Climate AI solutions often reinforce existing power imbalances, serving corporate interests through surveillance and commodification while neglecting social and environmental justice.
sub_domain: Earth for AI and Political Ecology Discussions

title: Future Schools and the Energy Implications of AI in Education: A Review of Scenarios and Method for Engaging Young People in Futures Thinking
description: Engaging students in future school scenarios fosters critical thinking on AI’s environmental impact, addressing gaps in policy and industry narratives.
sub_domain: Engaging the Next Generation with AI in Education

title: Enhancing Disease Detection in the Aquaculture Sector Using Convolutional Neural Networks Analysis
description: Convolutional neural networks enable accurate, automated fish disease detection, improving aquaculture sustainability and food security.
sub_domain: Modeling Disease Detection in the Aquaculture Sector

title: Artificial Intelligence Impact on the Environment: Hidden Ecological Costs and Ethical-Legal Issues
description: AI drives innovation across industries but carries a significant hidden ecological cost that must be addressed for sustainable development.
journal: Journal of Digital Technologies and Law
sub_domain: The Hidden Ecological Costs and Ethical-Legal Issues of AI

title: Quantifying Floating Plastic Debris at Sea Using Vessel-Based Optical Data and Artificial Intelligence
description: An object-detection algorithm improves offshore macroplastic quantification, aligning with global models and enhancing plastic pollution monitoring.
sub_domain: Modeling Plastic Debris at Sea Using AI

title: ClimaX: A Foundation Model for Weather and Climate
description: ClimaX, a deep learning model for weather and climate science, enhances forecasting and climate projections by integrating diverse datasets with a flexible Transformer-based architecture.
sub_domain: Evaluating of ClimaX with Weather and Climate

title: Time Series Predictions in Unmonitored Sites: A Survey of Machine Learning Techniques in Water Resources
description: Machine learning improves hydrological predictions in unmonitored sites, but integrating watershed characteristics and explainable AI remains a key challenge.
sub_domain: Using ML Techniques in Water Resources

title: A Beginner's Guide to Power and Energy Measurement and Estimation for Computing and Machine Learning
description: Accurate energy measurement is essential for reducing ML’s carbon footprint, requiring standardized tools, best practices, and improved adoption among researchers.
sub_domain: Power, Energy, and Estimating in Computing and ML

title: The Making of Critical Data Center Studies
description: Critical Data Center Studies examine data centers as social, political, and environmental entities, challenging their portrayal as mere infrastructure or "the cloud."
sub_domain: The Making of Data Centers

title: Unveiling Environmental Impacts of Large Language Model Serving: A Functional Unit View
description: FUEL (Functional Unit-based Evaluation of LLMs) introduces a standardized framework for assessing the environmental impact of LLMs, uncovering key trade-offs in model size, quantization, and hardware to optimize sustainability and reduce carbon emissions.
sub_domain: Using LLMs to Evaluate Environmental Impacts

title: Life-Cycle Emissions of AI Hardware: A Cradle-To-Grave Approach and Generational Trends
description: The first comprehensive life-cycle assessment (LCA) of AI accelerator greenhouse gas emissions publishes the first manufacturing emissions data for an AI accelerator, introduces the compute carbon intensity (CCI) metric, and proves a 3x improvement in CCI from TPU v4i to TPU v6e.
sub_domain: Analyzing Life-Cycle Emissions of AI Hardware

title: Evaluating Sustainability Reporting in SMEs: Insights From an Ethical Cooperative Bank’s Approach
description: This study aims to explore the integration of digital technologies in sustainability reporting practices for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) through the approach adopted by an Italian cooperative bank committed to ethical finance. It employs a dual evaluation framework combining traditional financial assessments with rigorous socio-environmental evaluations (VSA, Value and Sustainability Assessment) to ensure SMEs meet high social and environmental responsibility standards.
sub_domain: Evaluating Sustainability Reporting in Ethical Cooperation with Banks

title: Does ESG and Digital Transformation affects Corporate Sustainability? The Moderating Role of Green Innovation
description: ESG- and AI-based digital transformation enhances corporate sustainability by enabling eco-friendly operations, leveraging technological innovation to address societal and environmental challenges, and highlighting the long-term importance of green innovation, providing insights for businesses and policymakers.
sub_domain: Green Innovation in Corporate Sustainability and ESG

title: OpenForest: A Data Catalog for Machine Learning in Forest Monitoring
description: OpenForest is a dynamic, open-access catalog of 86 forest datasets across spatial scales, designed to support machine learning applications in forest monitoring by enhancing data-driven research, fostering collaborations, and addressing key challenges in forest biology and climate impact assessment.
sub_domain: OpenForest in Mapping Forest Biology and Climate Impact Assessments

title: Computational Advertising for Meaningful Brands, the Public Purpose, and a Sustainable Ecology: A Call for Research into a Systems Approach and Modeling Applications of LLMs in Marketing and Advertising
description: This paper critiques the failure of digital advertising to build brands and its broader societal impact while proposing a new AI-driven paradigm that redefines advertising’s goals around profit, people, and the planet through a systems dynamics framework.
journal: Journal of Current Issues & Research in Advertising
sub_domain: Examining AI-Driven Advertising Within the Triple Bottom Line (Profit, People, Planet)

title: Forecasting the Spread of Sustainability Movement: Computational Analysis on Social Media Messages Promoting Climate Actions
description: This study analyzes how social media messages about sustainability, specifically a UN climate-action movement from 2020–2022, influence public engagement, revealing that factors such as word count, hashtags, and visuals increase shares, while URLs, verified tags, and positive sentiment reduce engagement.
journal: Journal of Current Issues & Research in Advertising
sub_domain: Investigating How AI-Powered Analysis Helps Optimize Sustainability Messaging on Social Media for Maximum Public Engagement

title: Green Consumerism: A Cross-Cultural Linguistic and Sentiment Analysis of Sustainable Consumption Discourse on Twitter (X)
description: This study analyzes 72,000 tweets across the U.S., Switzerland, and India to reveal cultural differences in green consumerism, showing that Indian consumers emphasize environmental and social sustainability with altruistic values, while Western consumers focus more on economic aspects and egoistic values, offering insights for sustainability marketing and policy.
journal: Journal of Current Issues & Research in Advertising
sub_domain: Using AI to Analyze Consumer Sentiment and Values Across Different Cultures to Inform Sustainability Marketing and Policy Strategies

title: Effects of Eco-Labels and Perceived Influencer Expertise on Perceived Healthfulness, Perceived Product Quality, and Behavioral Intention
description: This study examines how the source of eco-labels (regulated vs. nonregulated) and influencer expertise (high vs. low) affect consumer perceptions of product quality, healthfulness, and advertising effectiveness, revealing that regulated eco-labels and credible influencers drive higher electronic word-of-mouth (eWOM) intentions.
journal: Journal of Current Issues & Research in Advertising
sub_domain: Investigating How AI-Enhanced Analysis of Eco-Labels and Influencer Credibility Impacts Consumer Trust and Purchase Behavior in Social Media Advertising.

title: Extracting Profits from the Public: How Utility Ratepayers Are Paying for Big Tech’s Power
description: The paper uncovers how utilities are forcing ratepayers to fund discounted rates for data centers. Martin and Peskoe explain that government-regulated utility rates socialize a utility’s costs of providing electricity service to the public.
sub_domain: How Utilities are Forcing Ratepayers to Fund Discounted Rates for Data Centers

title: SSL4EO-S12 v1.1: A Multimodal, Multiseasonal Dataset for Pretraining, Updated
description: SSL4EO-S12 v1.1 is an improved multimodal, multitemporal Earth Observation dataset designed for pretraining large-scale foundation models in geospatial AI. Building on v1.0, this version resolves misalignment issues and enhances data structure for easier, analysis-ready EO processing. It includes nearly one million patches covering the 10,000 largest cities and their surrounding 50 km radius across four seasons.
sub_domain: A Multimodal, Multitemporal Earth Observation Dataset Designed for Pretraining Large-Scale Foundation Models.

title: Application of Data Mining Techniques on Air Pollution of Dhaka City
description: This study applies machine learning, including deep learning with LSTM, to classify and predict Dhaka's Air Quality Index (AQI), incorporating daily temperature as a parameter and demonstrating optimal AQI forecasting performance.
sub_domain: Data Mining Techniques for Predicting Dhaka's Air Quality Index (AQI)

title: Artificial Intelligence and Robotics in the Hydrogen Lifecycle: A Systematic Review
description: This review analyzes AI and robotics applications in hydrogen production, storage, and transportation, highlighting their role in optimizing efficiency, safety, and scalability while identifying key challenges and future research directions.
sub_domain: AI and Robotics Applications in Hydrogen Production, Storage, and Transportation

title: To Crop or Not to Crop: Comparing Whole-Image and Cropped Classification on a Large Dataset of Camera Trap Images
description: This study evaluates whether incorporating object detection into camera trap image classification improves accuracy. Camera traps generate vast amounts of data, creating a processing bottleneck for conservation organizations. AI offers a solution, but current models struggle with small animals and generalizing across ecosystems.
sub_domain: Automated Wildlife Monitoring: Enhancing Camera Trap Image Classification with Object Detection

title: Climate AI for Corporate Decarbonization Metrics Extraction
description: This study presents the Climate Artificial Intelligence for Corporate Decarbonization Metrics Extraction (CAI) model, which leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the extraction and validation of corporate greenhouse gas (GHG) emission targets from public disclosures.
sub_domain: AI-Driven Automation for Extracting Corporate Greenhouse Gas Emission Metrics

title: Screening for Drinking Water Contaminants of Concern Using an Automated Exposure-Focused Workflow
description: The number of chemicals present in the environment exceeds the capacity of government bodies to characterize risk. Therefore, data-informed and reproducible processes are needed for identifying chemicals for further assessment.
sub_domain: Using Machine Learning for Exposure Screening of Drinking Water

title: Automated Fact-Checking of Climate Claims With Large Language Models
description: The study introduces Climinator, an AI-driven fact-checking framework that enhances climate claim verification by integrating authoritative sources and a debating approach, improving misinformation detection and informed policy discussions.
sub_domain: AI for Misinformation Detection in Climate Policy

title: SLAGREEF: Preliminary Results on Slag-Based Artificial Reef Design
description: The SLAGREEF project explores the use of slag furnace residues and inert waste to 3D-print artificial reefs, aiming to protect coastal areas and restore marine ecosystems, with ongoing monitoring at the Obsea underwater observatory.
sub_domain: AI & Sustainable Marine Infrastructure

title: Automating the Analysis of Fish Abundance Using Object Detection: Optimizing Animal Ecology With Deep Learning
description: This study demonstrates that deep learning outperforms human experts and citizen scientists in accurately and efficiently quantifying fish abundance from underwater footage, offering a faster and more cost-effective alternative for aquatic ecology monitoring.
sub_domain: AI & Automated Marine Biodiversity Monitoring

title: Sustainable Farming With Machine Learning Solutions for Minimizing Food Waste
description: This study examines how AI and ML can reduce food waste in the agricultural supply chain, demonstrating significant reductions in post-harvest losses and transportation waste while highlighting the need for further optimization in market demand prediction.
sub_domain: Use of Machine Learning to Minimize Food Waste and Optimize Resource Efficiency in Farming

title: Traffic Simulations: Multi-City Calibration of Metropolitan Highway Networks
description: This paper presents a novel travel demand calibration approach for high-resolution stochastic traffic simulators, leveraging abundant path-level travel times instead of traditional sensor counts, demonstrating scalable efficiency across six metropolitan highway networks and outperforming the SPSA algorithm by improving data fit by up to 80% with fewer simulation calls.
sub_domain: AI for Transportation With Traffic Simulation

title: Advancing Autonomous Vehicle Intelligence: Deep Learning and Multimodal LLM for Traffic Sign Recognition and Robust Lane Detection
description: This paper presents an integrated deep learning and Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) approach to enhance autonomous vehicle perception, achieving state-of-the-art traffic sign recognition and robust lane detection under various conditions, significantly improving AV navigation safety.
sub_domain: Multimodal Learning for Traffic Sign Detection and Lane Detection

title: Lightweight Multimodal Artificial Intelligence Framework for Maritime Multi-Scene Recognition
description: This paper presents a multimodal AI framework integrating image data, textual descriptions, and classification vectors from a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) to enhance maritime multi-scene recognition, achieving 98% accuracy while optimizing deployment for resource-constrained Autonomous Surface Vehicles (ASVs) in marine conservation, environmental monitoring, and disaster response.
sub_domain: Multimodal Detection for Maritime Applications with Intelligent Maritime Robotics

title: XAI4Extremes: An Interpretable Machine Learning Framework for Understanding Extreme-Weather Precursors Under Climate Change
description: This paper proposes a post-hoc interpretability framework to identify key extreme-weather precursors using deep learning models, comparing machine-generated relevance weather maps with existing domain knowledge to enhance understanding of climate change-driven extreme events, demonstrated through an analysis of Indochina heatwaves.
sub_domain: XAI4Extremes and Machine Learning Framework for Extreme Weather Scenarios with Climate Change

title: The Futures of Climate Modeling
description: This paper examines the current state and future directions of climate modeling, emphasizing the importance of leveraging diverse tools and methodologies by reviewing historical successes in Earth system predictions across timescales to guide meaningful progress.
sub_domain: Current State of Climate Modeling and Future Paths with Machine Learning

title: Decoding the Spatiotemporal Complexities of the Permafrost Carbon Feedback With Multimodal Ensemble Learning
description: This paper introduces GeoCryoAI, an AI-driven hybrid ensemble model that integrates in situ, remote sensing, and process-based data to quantify permafrost carbon dynamics in Alaska, demonstrating improved accuracy in predicting active layer thickness and greenhouse gas fluxes while addressing spatiotemporal disparities in permafrost degradation monitoring.
sub_domain: Permafrost Carbon Dynamics, Remote Sensing, and AI-Driven Climate Modeling

title: Intelligent Drone Design for Precision Cashew Farming
description: AI-powered drones equipped with advanced imaging and edge computing significantly enhance precision agriculture in cashew farming by enabling early disease detection and optimized pesticide application, achieving up to 95% accuracy in anthracnose classification and 99% accuracy in identifying healthy leaves, thereby improving sustainability, productivity, and resource efficiency in developing regions.
sub_domain: AI-Driven Crop Disease Management and Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) Applications in Agriculture

title: Coffee Disease Classification at the Edge Using Deep Learning
description: This study successfully embeds convolutional networks in a low-cost microcontrolled board to classify coffee leaf diseases with up to 98% accuracy, enabling early disease detection in coffee farms without internet access.
authors: João Vitor Yukio Bordin Yamashita, João Paulo R.R. Leite
sub_domain: Embedded Systems For Precision Agriculture Using Deep Learning-Based Plant Disease Detection

title: Classifying Mosquito Wingbeat Sound Using TinyML
description: This study presents a low-power, low-cost TinyML-based prototype for automatic mosquito species identification using acoustic detection, enabling large-scale vector-borne disease monitoring in resource-constrained areas with LoRaWAN communication.
sub_domain: Embedded Systems For Vector-Borne Disease Monitoring Using TinyML

title: A Low-Cost TinyML Model for Mosquito Detection in Resource-Constrained Environments
description: This study proposes a low-cost IoT-based TinyML model that intelligently activates mosquito repellent only when an Anopheles mosquito is detected, using a 1D-CNN for efficient classification of mosquito wingbeat sounds, reducing energy waste and potential health risks.
sub_domain: Embedded Systems For Smart Mosquito Control Using TinyML

title: Design and Development of a Family of Integrated Devices to Monitor Animal Movement in the Wild
description: This study presents a low-cost, low-power IoT-based monitoring device for tracking the vulnerable tortoise Chelonoidis chilensis in Argentina, featuring GNSS, RF transceiver, and multiple sensors, designed for minimal behavioral disturbance and adaptable for broader wildlife research.
sub_domain: Embedded Systems For Wildlife Monitoring And Conservation Using IoT

title: Recent Advances in Plant Diseases Detection With Machine Learning: Solution for Developing Countries
description: This study explores precision agriculture techniques for early plant disease and insect detection, comparing image processing with machine learning and deep learning approaches, and proposes a more accessible TinyML-based solution for low-cost, low-power implementation in developing countries.
sub_domain: Embedded Systems For Precision Agriculture Using TinyML

title: GreenTokenomics: Blockchain, AI, and Automation for Smarter Sustainability and Collaborative Ecosystem
description: This study proposes a blockchain-powered platform that connects SBTi certificate holders, SMEs, and green investors in a decentralized ecosystem, using digital rewards, AI-driven investor discovery, and automation to accelerate sustainability transitions and promote collaborative green initiatives.
sub_domain: Blockchain And AI For Sustainable Finance And Green Investments

title: V2X-LLM: Enhancing V2X Integration and Understanding in Connected Vehicle Corridors
description: This study introduces the V2X-LLM framework, which integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with Connected and Automated Vehicle (CAV) data pipelines to enhance real-time understanding, analysis, and decision-making in Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication, improving traffic safety, mobility, and optimization.
sub_domain: AI-Driven Intelligent Transportation Systems Using V2X And LLMs

title: Reinforcement Learning–Based Adaptive Strategies for Climate Change Adaptation: An Application for Coastal Flood Risk Management
description: This study demonstrates that reinforcement learning (RL) can enhance climate adaptation decision-making by dynamically optimizing coastal flood risk mitigation strategies for Manhattan, NYC, significantly reducing expected costs and improving resilience to uncertainties compared to conventional methods.
sub_domain: Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Climate Risk Management

title: Enhancing Maritime Domain Awareness Through AI-Enabled Acoustic Buoys for Real-Time Detection and Tracking of Fast-Moving Vessels
description: This study evaluates AI-enabled acoustic buoys equipped with OpenEar™ hydrophones in a sparse sensor network for detecting, classifying, localizing, and tracking fast-moving vessels, demonstrating their effectiveness in enhancing maritime domain awareness and informing scalable autonomous surveillance systems.
sub_domain: Maritime Sensing and Surveillance and Acoustic Target Recognition

title: Early Warning of Complex Climate Risk with Integrated Artificial Intelligence
description: This perspective highlights the transformative potential of AI, especially causal and foundation models, in building equitable, user-centered, and multi-hazard early warning systems that support long-term climate resilience through responsible, FATES-aligned innovation.
sub_domain: AI for Climate-Resilient Early Warning Systems

title: Environmental Large Language Model Evaluation (ELLE) Dataset: A Benchmark for Evaluating Generative AI Applications in Eco-Environment Domain
description: The ELLE dataset introduces the first benchmark for evaluating generative AI in ecological and environmental sciences, offering 1,130 categorized QA pairs to enable standardized, objective assessment and foster sustainable AI applications.
sub_domain: Evaluation of Generative AI in Environmental Science

title: AI-ML Techniques for Green Hydrogen: A Comprehensive Review
description: This paper reviews the integration of artificial intelligence and machine learning across the green hydrogen value chain, highlighting their transformative role in enhancing efficiency, safety, and sustainability from renewable energy optimization to smart distribution systems.
sub_domain: AI and Machine Learning Applications in the Green Hydrogen Value Chain

title: An IoT-Based Data Analysis System: A Case Study on Tomato Cultivation Under Different Irrigation Regimes
description: This study presents an IoT-based smart agriculture system, Agriware, used to monitor tomato plant responses to varying irrigation levels, revealing that 60% of the standard irrigation recommendation optimizes both plant health and water conservation.
sub_domain: Precision Irrigation and Water Management in Smart Agriculture

title: Extreme Heat Prediction Through Deep Learning and Explainable AI
description: This study develops and evaluates deep learning models enhanced with explainable AI techniques to predict extreme heat events in Pakistan, finding that LSTM models deliver high accuracy and reveal humidity and maximum temperature as key predictors.
sub_domain: Climate Informatics in Different Areas

title: Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Assessment of Urban Growth Impacts on Land Surface Temperature in a Hot Desert Climate: A Case Study of Baghdad City
description: This study uses random forest-based AI and Landsat imagery to assess how urban growth in Baghdad from 1985 to 2021 has significantly increased land surface temperatures, primarily due to reduced vegetation and moisture.
sub_domain: Urban Climate Modeling in Baghdad

title: Artificial Intelligence in Urban Science: Why Does It Matter?
description: This article advocates for a symbiotic integration of AI and urban science, termed the "New Urban Science", to build smarter, more equitable cities, emphasizing the need for interpretability, ethical collaboration, and societal context in AI applications.
sub_domain: AI for Sustainable Urban Planning

title: AI-Driven Solutions in Microfinance Banking: Enhancing Credit Scoring, Loan Management, and Risk Mitigation in Financially Vulnerable Markets
description: AI enhances microfinance by improving credit access, risk assessment, and fraud prevention, but raises concerns around bias, transparency, and regulation.
journal: The Critical Review of Social Science Studies
sub_domain: AI for Inclusive Finance & Risk Reduction

title: Machine Learning and GIS-Based Multi-Hazard Risk Modeling for Uttarakhand: Integrating Seismic, Landslide, and Flood Susceptibility With Socioeconomic Vulnerability
description: This study uses machine learning and GIS to model multi-hazard risk in Uttarakhand by integrating seismic, landslide, and flood hazards with socioeconomic and built environment vulnerabilities.
sub_domain: AI for Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Resilience

title: Critical Misalignments in Climate Pledges Reveal Imbalanced Sustainable Development Pathways
description: The study examines how different income groups prioritize distinct Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in their climate action plans, revealing divergent development strategies and implications for global sustainable development.
sub_domain: Climate Policy and Sustainable Development Governance

title: AI-Driven Future Farming: Achieving Climate-Smart and Sustainable Agriculture
description: The study examines how emerging technologies like AI, IoT, machine learning, and remote sensing are revolutionizing agriculture by enabling precision farming, optimizing resource use, and addressing challenges of food production under climatic uncertainty.
sub_domain: Precision Agriculture and Agricultural Informatics

title: AI-Enhanced Decision-Making for Sustainable Supply Chains: Reducing Carbon Footprints in the USA
description: The research explores how artificial intelligence technologies can enhance supply chain sustainability in the United States by improving decision-making, reducing carbon emissions, and addressing regulatory and consumer expectations.
sub_domain: Sustainable Supply Chain Management and AI Technologies

title: Efficacy of Machine Learning in Simulating Precipitation and Its Extremes Over the Capital Cities in North Indian States
description: This study demonstrates that machine learning models, particularly Random Forest Classifiers, can effectively simulate and classify precipitation and extreme weather patterns across North Indian states, offering valuable insights for disaster preparedness and water resource management.
sub_domain: Machine Learning Applications in Climate Extremes Prediction

title: Large Language Models in Climate and Sustainability Policy: Limits and Opportunities
description: This study explores how large language models can effectively process and analyze climate and sustainability policy documents to support actionable insights, while highlighting key challenges in human oversight and real-world policy application.
sub_domain: AI for Climate Policy Analysis

title: Leveraging Remote Sensing for Optimized National Scale Agricultural Water Management in South Africa
description: This study used MODIS remote sensing data to analyze spatiotemporal variations in Crop Water Use (CWU) and Crop Water Productivity (CWP) across irrigated and rainfed croplands in South Africa from 2017 to 2022, highlighting regional differences and emphasizing the potential of remote sensing for optimizing agricultural water management.
sub_domain: Remote Sensing Applications in Agricultural Water Management

title: AI for Ocean Conservation: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Marine Sustainability
description: This study explores the role of AI for ocean conservation measures and the rise of marine sustainability.
sub_domain: Marine Sustainability and the Rise of AI in Ocean Science

title: Quantifying the Carbon Emissions of Machine Learning
description: This study introduces the Machine Learning Emissions Calculator, a tool designed to help the community estimate and mitigate the carbon emissions associated with training neural networks by considering factors such as server location, energy grid, training duration, and hardware type.
sub_domain: AI & Carbon Footprint Reduction

title: Carbontracker: Tracking and Predicting the Carbon Footprint of Training Deep Learning Models
description: This study introduces Carbontracker, a tool that tracks and predicts the energy and carbon footprint of training deep learning models, encouraging practitioners to report these metrics alongside model performance to promote responsible and energy-efficient computing.
sub_domain: AI & Carbon Footprint Reduction

title: The Carbon Impact of Artificial Intelligence
description: The part that artificial intelligence plays in climate change has come under scrutiny, including from tech workers themselves who joined the global climate strike last year. Much can be done by developing tools to quantify the carbon cost of machine learning models and by switching to a sustainable artificial intelligence infrastructure.
sub_domain: The Carbon Footprint of AI

title: Green AI
description: Creating efficiency in AI research will decrease its carbon footprint and increase its inclusivity as deep learning study should not require the deepest pockets.
sub_domain: Green AI and Environmental Implications

title: Towards the Systematic Reporting of the Energy and Carbon Footprints of Machine Learning
description: This study introduces a framework that simplifies real-time tracking of energy consumption and carbon emissions in machine learning research, includes a leaderboard to promote energy-efficient reinforcement learning, and proposes strategies for mitigating carbon emissions and reducing energy usage.
sub_domain: AI Footprint with Carbon Emissions and Energy Consumption

title: The Energy and Carbon Footprint of Training End-to-End Speech Recognizers
description: This study quantifies the carbon cost of training state-of-the-art automatic speech recognition (ASR) systems, revealing that minimal performance improvements often come at a disproportionately high carbon cost, and provides guidelines to help researchers assess and mitigate the environmental impact of training speech technologies.
sub_domain: AI and Environmental Impact Quantification

title: Carbon Emissions and Large Neural Network Training
description: This study estimates the energy use and carbon footprint of several large models, highlights opportunities to improve energy efficiency through model sparsity, workload scheduling, and datacenter optimization, and advocates for including energy usage and CO2 equivalent emissions (CO2e) as key metrics in evaluating machine learning models.
sub_domain: AI & Carbon Footprint Reduction

title: The Earth Alignment Principle for Artificial Intelligence
description: At a time when the world must cut greenhouse gas emissions precipitously, artificial intelligence (AI) brings large opportunities and large risks. To address its uncertain environmental impact, we propose the ‘Earth alignment’ principle to guide AI development and deployment towards planetary stability.
sub_domain: The Earth Alignment Principle and AI

title: Greener Principles for Environmentally Sustainable Computational Science
description: The carbon footprint of scientific computing is substantial, but environmentally sustainable computational science (ESCS) is a nascent field with many opportunities to thrive.
sub_domain: Greener Computational Science Principles

title: Urban Planning and IPCC-Like City Assessments Integration for Climate-Resilient Cities
description: Integrating IPCC frameworks with urban planning can help cities become climate-resilient hubs for innovation and equity.
sub_domain: Urban Climate Resilience and Policy Integration

title: Skills or Degree? The Rise of Skill-Based Hiring for AI and Green Jobs
description: Employers in AI roles are shifting toward skill-based hiring in the green field, reducing emphasis on formal degrees while valuing AI skills with a 23% wage premium.
sub_domain: Green Skills and AI Development

title: The Carbon Footprint of Machine Learning Training Will Plateau, Then Shrink
description: Machine learning (ML) workloads have rapidly grown, raising concerns about their carbon footprint. We show four best practices to reduce ML training energy and carbon dioxide emissions. If the whole ML field adopts best practices, we predict that by 2030, total carbon emissions from training will decline.
sub_domain: Carbon Footprint of Training AI Models

title: Global Prediction of Extreme Floods in Ungauged Watersheds
description: AI-based flood forecasting models predict extreme riverine events in ungauged watersheds with similar or better reliability than current global systems, providing earlier and more accurate warnings for high-impact floods across 80+ countries.
sub_domain: AI Applications in Disaster Risk Management

title: Transformer-Based Fast Mole Fraction of CO2 Retrievals from Satellite-Measured Spectra
description: A new Transformer-based neural network model called Spectrum Transformer (SpT) enables fast, accurate, and generalizable satellite-based CO₂ monitoring, significantly improving computational efficiency and real-time applicability over traditional retrieval methods.
sub_domain: AI for Satellite-Based Climate Monitoring

title: Single-Node Power Demand During AI Training: Measurements on an 8-GPU NVIDIA H100 System
description: This study measures the energy use of an 8-GPU NVIDIA H100 node during AI model training, showing that larger batch sizes can significantly reduce total energy consumption, offering valuable insights for data center energy planning and future sustainability efforts.
sub_domain: AI Infrastructure & Energy Efficiency

title: NNetNav: Unsupervised Learning of Browser Agents Through Environment Interaction in the Wild
description: NNetNav is a method for training browser agents without human supervision by generating synthetic demonstrations through structured web exploration, significantly improving performance on web interaction benchmarks and setting a new state-of-the-art for unsupervised approaches.
sub_domain: AI for Automated Web Interaction & Environmental Data Access

title: Climate Sustainability through AI-Crypto Synergies and Energy Transition in the Digital Landscape to Cut 0.7 GtCO2e by 2030
description: This study models the integration of AI data centers and cryptocurrency mining with shared renewable infrastructure to enable climate-neutral digital operations, showing potential for significant CO₂ reductions and advocating for supportive global policy frameworks.
sub_domain: AI, Cryptocurrency & Sustainable Energy Systems

title: Skilful Global Seasonal Predictions From a Machine Learning Weather Model Trained on Reanalysis Data
description: A machine learning weather model, ACE2, trained on reanalysis data, demonstrates skillful global seasonal forecasting, comparable to leading physics-based models—especially in predicting the North Atlantic Oscillation, suggesting ML's potential in near-term climate prediction.
sub_domain: AI & Climate Forecasting

title: The Chalmers Cloud Ice Climatology: A Novel Robust Climate Record of Frozen Cloud Hydrometeor Concentrations
description: The Chalmers Cloud Ice Climatology (CCIC) provides a highly accurate, 40-year global dataset of total ice water path, bridging gaps between existing records and enhancing our understanding of cloud ice trends and storm dynamics in a changing climate.
sub_domain: AI & Atmospheric Monitoring

title: Climate Adaptation Plans and “Green Jobs”: Challenges for Implementing a Responsive, Multi-Skilled Workforce in Worcester, Massachusetts
description: Using natural language processing and interviews, this study finds that vague definitions and geographic inaccessibility of green jobs in Worcester, MA hinder the development of a climate-ready workforce and the implementation of local climate adaptation plans.
sub_domain: AI & Green Workforce Development

title: Combining Climate Models and Observations to Predict the Time Remaining Until Regional Warming Thresholds Are Reached
description: This study uses a convolutional neural network enhanced with transfer learning to combine climate models and observations, enabling more accurate regional predictions for when specific warming thresholds will be reached, with many regions likely to exceed 1.5 °C by 2040.
sub_domain: AI for Climate Forecasting & Modeling

title: Significantly Wetter or Drier Future Conditions for One to Two Thirds of the World’s Population
